text: Understand Anything is an open-source plugin that maps an entire codebase into a visual, interactive knowledge graph with one-line installation across 14 platforms.

evidence.quote: This is an open-source plugin that maps your entire codebase into a visual, interactive knowledge graph.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The multi-agent pipeline scans every file, extracts every function, class, import, and dependency, and assembles it into a knowledge graph saved as JSON.

evidence.quote: A multi-agent pipeline scans every file, extracts every function, class, import, and dependency, and assembles it into a knowledge graph saved as JSON.

text: The five parallel agents are: project-scanner (discovers all files, detects languages and frameworks), file-analyzer (extracts functions, classes, imports; builds graph nodes and edges), architecture-analyzer (identifies architectural layers), tour-builder (generates guided learning tours ordered by dependency), and graph-reviewer (validates graph completeness and referential integrity).

evidence.quote: project-scanner - Discovers all files, detects languages and frameworks

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p036.s001`..`p038.s001` around the ref
4 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: This activates a 6th agent (domain-analyzer) that maps your code to business processes: domains, flows, and steps.

evidence.quote: This activates a 6th agent (domain-analyzer) that maps your code to business processes: domains, flows, and steps.

text: The structural graph (/understand) maps code: files, functions, classes, imports, and layers, while the domain graph (/understand-domain) maps business logic: domains, flows, and process steps as separate views of the same codebase.

evidence.quote: The structural graph (/understand) maps code: files, functions, classes, imports, layers.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Incremental updates work by using Tree-sitter to generate a fingerprint per file during the initial scan, so that subsequent /understand runs only re-analyze files whose fingerprint has changed.

evidence.quote: Tree-sitter generates a fingerprint per file during the initial scan.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The codebase is 200,000 lines.

evidence.quote: The codebase is 200,000 lines.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Existing tools (Sourcegraph, Greptile, GitNexus, DeepWiki) solve parts of the problem but none provide a visual, interactive, shareable, agent-integrated knowledge graph covering structural analysis, business domain mapping, guided architectural tours, and knowledge-base analysis in a single plugin workflow.

evidence.quote: None of them give you a visual, interactive, shareable, agent-integrated knowledge graph.

text: The plugin requires no npm installs, config files, or environment setup beyond installation.

evidence.quote: No npm installs, no config files, no environment setup.

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p024.s003`..`p025.s002` around the ref

text: File analysis runs in batches of 20-30 files with up to 5 concurrent analyzers.

evidence.quote: File analysis runs in batches of 20–30 files, up to 5 concurrent.

text: The architecture-analyzer groups nodes into layers (API, Service, Data, UI, Utility) by analyzing file structure and what each module exports and imports.

evidence.quote: The architecture-analyzer groups nodes into layers (API, Service, Data, UI, Utility)

text: The tour-builder topologically sorts the graph to order the guided tour from foundational modules to high-level ones.

evidence.quote: The tour-builder topologically sorts the graph — ordering the guided tour from foundational modules to high-level ones

text: LLM costs are non-trivial for large repos with many files, and no published cost estimate exists for average codebase sizes.

evidence.quote: Large repos with many files will accumulate non-trivial token costs.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Graph quality mirrors code quality.

evidence.quote: Graph quality mirrors code quality.

text: There is no published independent benchmark for graph accuracy regarding how often summaries are correct, architectural layer assignments are accurate, or guided tours compare to human-written walkthroughs.

evidence.quote: No published independent benchmark for graph accuracy.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Understand Anything does not expose the knowledge graph via MCP for AI agents; the graph is human-facing and agents interact via slash commands instead of a programmatic interface.

evidence.quote: It does not expose an MCP server for programmatic agent consumption.

evidence.quote: /understand-knowledge is under-documented.

text: For large monorepos, use subdirectory scoping like /understand src/frontend or /understand packages/api to avoid long initial scan times and higher LLM costs.

evidence.quote: Yes, with subdirectory scoping: /understand src/frontend or /understand packages/api.

text: The graph is stored entirely locally in .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json in the repo, and can be committed and shared with teammates.

evidence.quote: The graph lives in .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json in your repo, so entirely local.

text: Cursor manual install fallback noted in README. Auto-discovery via .cursor-plugin/plugin.json doesn't always fire; the README now explicitly documents the manual fallback as of yesterday's commit.

evidence.quote: Cursor manual install fallback noted in README. Auto-discovery via .cursor-plugin/plugin.json doesn't always fire; the README now explicitly documents the manual fallback as of yesterday's commit.

text: No MCP server integration. Unlike GitNexus, Understand Anything does not expose the knowledge graph via MCP for agent consumption.

evidence.quote: No MCP server integration. Unlike GitNexus, Understand Anything does not expose the knowledge graph via MCP for agent consumption.

text: The architecture-analyzer groups nodes into layers (API, Service, Data, UI, Utility) by analyzing the file structure and the nature of what each module exports and imports.

evidence.quote: The architecture-analyzer groups nodes into layers (API, Service, Data, UI, Utility) by analyzing the file structure and the nature of what each module exports and imports.

text: Poorly named functions, no separation of concerns, and large procedural scripts produce noisy graphs.

evidence.quote: Poorly named functions, no separation of concerns, and large procedural scripts produce noisy graphs.

text: For the knowledge base use case (/understand-knowledge), there's a deterministic parser that extracts wikilinks and categories from index.md, combined with LLM agents that discover implicit relationships and extract claims.

evidence.quote: For the knowledge base use case (/understand-knowledge), there's a deterministic parser that extracts wikilinks and categories from index.md, combined with LLM agents that discover implicit relationships and extract claims.

text: The domain view is more useful for PMs and business stakeholders; the structural view is more useful for engineers.

evidence.quote: The domain view is more useful for PMs and business stakeholders; the structural view is more useful for engineers.
